title: Implement Token Bucket Rate Limiting for API Endpoints
statement:
Create a backend API with token bucket rate limiting to control the number of requests per second from clients. Implement a single endpoint to return a success message when a client's IP address meets the rate limit requirements.

You must use the Express framework with the `express-rate-limit` middleware to implement the token bucket algorithm. 

Your API should listen on port 3000 and have a single endpoint at `/api/rate-limited-endpoint` that responds with a JSON body containing a success message. The endpoint should be rate-limited to 5 requests per second for each client IP address.

To test your API, you can use the provided test cases. Your API should pass all the test cases to receive full points.

Note: You must use the `express-rate-limit` middleware to implement rate limiting. You cannot use any other rate limiting solution or external libraries.

**Endpoint Requirements**

* `GET /api/rate-limited-endpoint`:
  * Responds with a JSON body containing a success message.
  * Rate-limited to 5 requests per second for each client IP address.
  * Must use the `express-rate-limit` middleware to implement rate limiting.

**Test Cases**

The provided test cases will be used to verify the correctness of your API. Make sure your API passes all the test cases to receive full points.

**Constraints**

* Must use the `express-rate-limit` middleware to implement rate limiting.
* Must listen on port 3000.
* Must have a single endpoint at `/api/rate-limited-endpoint` that responds with a JSON body containing a success message.
